abstract.para: You can edit primitive resources with the &crmshell; (&crmsh;) or with the &hawk; Web interface.

step.para: Log in either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
To add a new monitor operation, use the following command:
To add a new parameter, meta attribute or utilization attribute, use one of the following commands:
step.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm resource param RESOURCE set PARAMETER VALUE &prompt.user;sudo crm resource meta RESOURCE set ATTRIBUTE VALUE &prompt.user;sudo crm resource utilization RESOURCE set ATTRIBUTE VALUE

step.para: To delete a parameter, meta attribute or utilization attribute, use one of the following commands:
step.screen: &prompt.user;sudo crm resource param RESOURCE delete PARAMETER &prompt.user;sudo crm resource meta RESOURCE delete ATTRIBUTE &prompt.user;sudo crm resource utilization RESOURCE delete ATTRIBUTE

step.para: To change multiple attributes at once, use the following command:
This command opens the CIB representation of the resource in a text editor. You can make multiple changes to the resource's configuration, then save and exit.

important.para: Don't use this command to rename a resource. The change won't apply to related objects.

step.para: To rename a resource, first stop the resource, then use the following command:
This also changes the resource's name in related objects such as constraints, groups and clones.
In a Web browser, go to https://HAWKSERVER:7630/ and log in.
From the left navigation bar, select Configuration Edit Configuration.
Find the resource you want to edit. In the Operations column, click the Edit icon.
At the top of the configuration screen, you can select the following operations:

listitem.para: Rename the resource. The resource must be stopped before you perform this operation.

step.para: To add a new parameter, operation or meta attribute, select an item from one of the drop-down lists.
To delete a parameter, operation or meta attribute, click the minus icon.
To edit an existing parameter, enter a new value in the text field.
To edit an existing meta attribute, select a new value from the drop-down list.
To edit an existing operation, click the Edit icon.
A new window opens. Enter a different value or add a new attribute, then click Apply to close the window.
If you make a mistake, you can click Revert to undo your changes. This option is only available before you confirm the changes.
Click Apply to confirm the changes.
A message at the top of the screen shows if the action was successful.
